title: GetResponse vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing platform for your needs.
h1: GetResponse vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse excels for online course creators and webinar hosts with advanced automation, while Constant Contact specializes in serving nonprofits and local businesses with straightforward tools. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.

comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social ads, e-commerce

choose_a_if: You want a free tier to test the platform before paying
You need webinar hosting, landing page builders, or online course functionality
You're building complex marketing automation workflows and conversion funnels
choose_b_if: You run a nonprofit or local business seeking straightforward email marketing
You need e-commerce integration and SMS marketing capabilities
You want event management and social media advertising features

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: GetResponse is better for advanced users needing webinars, courses, and automation, while Constant Contact excels for small businesses and nonprofits seeking simplicity. The choice depends on your specific business model and feature requirements rather than one being objectively superior.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Constant Contact?
faq.a: GetResponse offers a free tier for up to 500 subscribers, making it cheaper to start. Constant Contact's lowest paid plan begins at $12/month with no free option. However, once you exceed GetResponse's free limit, the $19/month plan may be comparable or more expensive depending on subscriber count.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports, making migration possible. Most email marketing platforms allow you to export your contacts and import them into GetResponse. We recommend testing GetResponse's free tier with a portion of your list before committing to a full migration.
